message:
feat(quic): add cancellation and region-lifecycle correlation to forensic log (T4.8)

Add CancelRequested and RegionStateChanged event variants to the QUIC/H3
forensic logging system to enable deterministic incident forensics and
replay triage for cancellation scenarios.

Key additions:
- CancelRequested event captures scope, reason, and trigger for cancel signals
- RegionStateChanged event tracks region_id, state transitions, live children,
  and outstanding obligations
- CancelRegionSummary struct correlates first-cancel to first-close latency
- extract_cancel_region() populates the summary from forensic records
- Manifest now includes cancel_region_summary alongside transport/h3 summaries

The motivation is that cancellation-to-close latency is a critical diagnostic
signal for QUIC connection lifecycle debugging. Without explicit correlation,
operators must manually reconstruct cancel/drain timelines from raw event logs.

Also updates capability evidence map and risk register to document T4.8 closure,
removes an unnecessary .clone() in quic_h3_e2e test, and adds forensic log
surface assertions to the capability inventory test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -190,7 +190,7 @@ Counts are approximate (±10%) from automated `#[test]`/`#[cfg(test)]` scanning.
 | Tests (inline) | Source files in quic_native, quic_core, h3_native |
 | Tests (integration) | `tests/quic_h3_e2e.rs`, `tests/quic_h3_e2e_cancel.rs`, `tests/quic_h3_e2e_loss.rs`, `tests/quic_h3_e2e_h3.rs`, `tests/quic_h3_e2e_violations.rs` |
 | Test count | ~472 |
-| Notes | T4.2/T4.3 transport parity is closed across handshake lifecycle, stream transitions, loss-recovery, close semantics, and flow/congestion stress correctness (see `net/quic_native/{connection,transport}.rs` plus violation/loss E2E suites). T4.4 extends this with native key-update lifecycle coverage and explicit 0-RTT/resumption + migration guard paths (`enable_resumption_0rtt`, `request_path_migration`, `set_active_migration_disabled`) exercised in `tests/quic_h3_e2e.rs`. T4.5 extends native H3/QPACK integration with validated decode helpers (`qpack_decode_request_field_section`, `qpack_decode_response_field_section`) and malformed peer pseudo-header ordering coverage in `tests/quic_h3_e2e_violations.rs` (`h3_qpack_request_pseudo_after_regular_header_is_rejected`). RFC 9002 remains partially implemented beyond this closure; compatibility wrappers remain explicitly separated and disabled by default. |
+| Notes | T4.2/T4.3 transport parity is closed across handshake lifecycle, stream transitions, loss-recovery, close semantics, and flow/congestion stress correctness (see `net/quic_native/{connection,transport}.rs` plus violation/loss E2E suites). T4.4 extends this with native key-update lifecycle coverage and explicit 0-RTT/resumption + migration guard paths (`enable_resumption_0rtt`, `request_path_migration`, `set_active_migration_disabled`) exercised in `tests/quic_h3_e2e.rs`. T4.5 extends native H3/QPACK integration with validated decode helpers (`qpack_decode_request_field_section`, `qpack_decode_response_field_section`) and malformed peer pseudo-header ordering coverage in `tests/quic_h3_e2e_violations.rs` (`h3_qpack_request_pseudo_after_regular_header_is_rejected`). T4.8 extends `net/quic_native/forensic_log.rs` with cancellation/region correlation signals (`CancelRequested`, `RegionStateChanged`, `cancel_region_summary`) for deterministic incident forensics and replay triage. RFC 9002 remains partially implemented beyond this closure; compatibility wrappers remain explicitly separated and disabled by default. |
 
 ### F16 — Web Framework

@@ -67,7 +67,7 @@ Higher values indicate higher urgency for remediation.
 | F12 | TLS | M3 | R2 | D2 | 134 | 4 | rustls-only (no native-tls). Feature-gated, well-tested. |
 | F13 | WebSocket | M3 | R2 | D2 | 185 | 4 | Native impl (no tungstenite). Good test coverage. |
 | F14 | HTTP/1+2 | M3 | R2 | D2 | 717 | 4 | Native impl. Pre-existing compile error in h2/connection.rs (in-flight fix by another agent). |
-| F15 | QUIC + HTTP/3 | M1 | **R4** | D1 | 472 | **48** | **CRITICAL.** T4.2/T4.3 transport parity is closed (handshake lifecycle, stream transitions, loss-recovery, flow/congestion stress correctness) and native `quic`/`http3` surfaces are exposed. T4.4 hardens key-update lifecycle plus native 0-RTT/resumption and migration guards in `quic_native` state machines. T4.5 extends native HTTP/3/QPACK integration with validated request/response head decode paths and pseudo-header ordering/duplication enforcement for malformed peer field sections. Remaining blocker is broad interop matrix closure. Legacy quinn/h3 wrappers remain compat-only and off by default due Tokio-transitive risk. |
+| F15 | QUIC + HTTP/3 | M1 | **R4** | D1 | 472 | **48** | **CRITICAL.** T4.2/T4.3 transport parity is closed (handshake lifecycle, stream transitions, loss-recovery, flow/congestion stress correctness) and native `quic`/`http3` surfaces are exposed. T4.4 hardens key-update lifecycle plus native 0-RTT/resumption and migration guards in `quic_native` state machines. T4.5 extends native HTTP/3/QPACK integration with validated request/response head decode paths and pseudo-header ordering/duplication enforcement for malformed peer field sections. T4.8 extends forensic observability with explicit cancellation and region-lifecycle correlation summaries (`CancelRequested`, `RegionStateChanged`, `cancel_region_summary`) for replay diagnostics. Remaining blocker is broad interop matrix closure. Legacy quinn/h3 wrappers remain compat-only and off by default due Tokio-transitive risk. |
 | F16 | Web Framework | M3 | R2 | D2 | 111 | 4 | Missing: multipart, SSE, cookie extraction, CORS. Moderate gap. |
 | F17 | gRPC | M3 | R2 | D2 | 235 | 4 | Native impl (no tonic). Good coverage for unary/streaming. |
 | F18 | Database | M3 | R2 | D2 | 168 | 4 | Feature-gated. Missing: connection pool abstraction. Wire protocols present. |
